tossups.question: An artist who worked in this style included two maids huddling around Elizabeth and Mary in his version of The Visitation. The first woman artist documented to have painted female nudes, Lavinia Fontana, primarily painted in this style. Sweden’s Skokloster Castle houses a portrait painted in a “Northern” form of this style that depicts Rudolf II as an assemblage of vegetables. Cupid cradles Venus’s (*) breasts in a work in this style; that work also depicts pink petals being clutched by Folly. Mary, who poses figura serpentinata, cradles an oversized baby Jesus in a painting in this style titled Madonna with a Long Neck. For 10 points, what late Renaissance art style exemplified by the exaggeration of bodily features was practiced by Parmagianino?
tossups.question_sanitized: An artist who worked in this style included two maids huddling around Elizabeth and Mary in his version of The Visitation. The first woman artist documented to have painted female nudes, Lavinia Fontana, primarily painted in this style. Sweden's Skokloster Castle houses a portrait painted in a "Northern" form of this style that depicts Rudolf II as an assemblage of vegetables. Cupid cradles Venus's (*) breasts in a work in this style; that work also depicts pink petals being clutched by Folly. Mary, who poses figura serpentinata, cradles an oversized baby Jesus in a painting in this style titled Madonna with a Long Neck. For 10 points, what late Renaissance art style exemplified by the exaggeration of bodily features was practiced by Parmagianino?
tossups.answer: Mannerism [or Mannerist; accept Northern Mannerism; accept late Renaissance until “late Renaissance” is read; prompt on maniera; reject “Renaissance”] (The painting in the first line was created by Jacopo da Pontormo.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Mannerism [or Mannerist; accept Northern Mannerism; accept late Renaissance until "late Renaissance" is read; prompt on maniera; reject "Renaissance"] (The painting in the first line was created by Jacopo da Pontormo.)

bonuses.leadin: Bill Viola recreated a painting by this artist as a video piece in which a pregnant woman whispers, “Can you help me?” to an older woman. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Renaissance artist of The Visitation, who depicted a crowd of figures clad in pale blues, oranges, and pinks surrounding the body of Christ in his Deposition from the Cross.

Pontormo’s The Visitation copies the composition of its four figures from this artist’s Four Witches. This German artist signed engravings like Knight, Death and the Devil with his trademark “AD” monogram.

Pontormo’s Portrait of a Halbedier has been claimed to depict this leader as a youth. Pontormo’s adopted son, Bronzino, painted this ruler’s wife wearing an ornately-brocaded dress and served as his court painter.
answers: Cosimo de’ Medici [or Cosimo I de’ Medici, Grand Duke of Tuscany; or Cosimo I; prompt on Cosimo; prompt on Medici]
answers_sanitized: Jacopo Pontormo [or Jacopo da Pontormo or Jacopo Carrucci]
Cosimo de' Medici [or Cosimo I de' Medici, Grand Duke of Tuscany; or Cosimo I; prompt on Cosimo; prompt on Medici]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Bill Viola recreated a painting by this artist as a video piece in which a pregnant woman whispers, "Can you help me?" to an older woman. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Renaissance artist of The Visitation, who depicted a crowd of figures clad in pale blues, oranges, and pinks surrounding the body of Christ in his Deposition from the Cross.

Pontormo's Portrait of a Halbedier has been claimed to depict this leader as a youth. Pontormo's adopted son, Bronzino, painted this ruler's wife wearing an ornately-brocaded dress and served as his court painter.

bonuses.leadin: This work is interrupted by a central oculus, above which are two putti perched on branches, each holding a golden standard, while two more putti appear seated on a wall below, holding up a garland. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting that occupies a lunette in the Medici villa at Poggio a Caiano. Both of the title figures are depicted as rustic peasants, one holding a sickle and the other handling a basket of fruits next to a thin dog.
Vertumnus and Pomona was painted by this Mannerist painter, who showed a child and his father ascending a spiral staircase to be blessed by the dying Jacob in one part of his Joseph in Egypt series.

Pontormo’s most famous work, located in the Capponi Chapel, depicts this scene, in which the body of Christ is surrounded by figures in soft blue and pink clothes. Rubens paired this scene with The Raising of the Cross.
answers_sanitized: Deposition [accept descent from the Cross and equivalents]

Pontormo's most famous work, located in the Capponi Chapel, depicts this scene, in which the body of Christ is surrounded by figures in soft blue and pink clothes. Rubens paired this scene with The Raising of the Cross.

bonuses.leadin: Vasari relates that this artist was tremendously afraid of death and avoided large gatherings at all costs. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Florentine painter, whose most famous work lies above the altar in the Capponi Chapel and depicts Mary and several women swooning as the limp body of Christ is lowered to the ground.
Pontormo painted in this 16th century Renaissance style characterized by exaggerated physical features. It was supplanted by Baroque art.
This Mannerist painter of the Bardi Altarpiece exaggerated the size of both the infant Jesus and Mary in his Madonna With a Long Neck.
answers_sanitized: Jacopo Pontormo [or Jacopo da Pontormo; or Jacopo Carucci]
Parmigianino [or Girolamo Francesco Maria Mazzola; or Parmigiano]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Vasari relates that this artist was tremendously afraid of death and avoided large gatherings at all costs. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this Florentine painter, whose most famous work lies above the altar in the Capponi Chapel and depicts Mary and several women swooning as the limp body of Christ is lowered to the ground.
Pontormo painted in this 16th century Renaissance style characterized by exaggerated physical features. It was supplanted by Baroque art.
This Mannerist painter of the Bardi Altarpiece exaggerated the size of both the infant Jesus and Mary in his Madonna With a Long Neck.

bonuses.leadin: Mary stands in a jubilant contrapposto pose in this man's wall fresco of the Annunciation, which like his most famous work is located in the Barbadori/Capponi Chapel. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this painter of a famous lunette showing Vertumnus and Pomona, who was cast by Vasari as an obsessive nutter who worked himself to death trying to finish his Last Judgement.
Pontormo is best known for his Mannerist rendering of this scene, which shoes Christ's serpentine body winding through the billowing blue and pink cloaks of his followers.

The Barbadori/Capponi Chapel housing Pontormo's Deposition was designed by this famed Renaissance architect, who saved Florence from centuries of humiliation after devising a way to put the dome on its cathedral.
answers: Deposition from the Cross [accept equivalents; do NOT accept “Crucifixion”]

bonuses.leadin: This painting is located above the altarpiece of the Capponi Chapel, and includes a crouching curly-haired figure in the foreground somehow supporting his weight with the tips of his toes. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this religious painting, which depicts a crowd of entangled blue and pink figures performing the title action.
The Deposition from the Cross was painted by this Mannerist from the Florentine school, who often worked with his student Bronzino.
Other Mannerists included El Greco, who painted this landscape which depicts a flashing storm bursting in the sky above the central location, whose hills zigzag towards the foreground.
answers: The Deposition from the Cross [or Descent from the Cross; or The Entombment]
answers_sanitized: The Deposition from the Cross [or Descent from the Cross; or The Entombment]
